title: Neurocallis scandens
taxonomy: Neurocallis scandens is classified as a species within the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard-apple family of flowering plants. It is native to Australia and New Guinea. Neurocallis scandens is a climbing shrub with yellow flowers and small, edible fruit. The plant is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Neurocallis scandens is considered to be rare and is listed as an endangered species.

intro: Neurocallis scandens, also known as Scandent Vine, is a woody vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to alleviate various health issues, including inflammation and infections, due to its reported antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neurocallis scandens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Neurocallis scandens is commonly prepared into herbal infusions and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts, to harness its purported health benefits.
